description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular stone block structure composed of three distinct tiers, each with a slightly different width, creating a stepped profile. The surface is covered in a repeating cobblestone texture, giving the appearance of interlocking, roughly hexagonal stones in muted beige and gray tones. The topmost tier is the smallest and flattest, while the base is the widest, providing stability and a solid foundation. The edges are straight and well-defined, with no visible signs of chipping or major wear. The block appears solid and heavy, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment. There are no visible engravings, openings, or interactive components, emphasizing its function as a static, supportive structure.
description: A stepped, rectangular stone block with a cobblestone texture in beige and gray, featuring three distinct tiers and a solid, heavy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the stepped profile with all three tiers clearly visible and the cobblestone pattern prominent.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the same stepped structure and stone texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same three-tiered, blocky form and consistent stone pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view matches the other side, with the same stepped design and cobblestone surface.
attribute_installation_method: set on a leveled concrete or compacted gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and uniformly textured with no moss, chips, or erosion.
